what_it_does: Defines a fixed 1600x900 HTML presentation view with a warm left split zone containing an SVG staged timeline and a right content area with headings, lead bullets, and grouped market statistics.
observations: The layout is a two-column grid with a 42% left zone and 58% right content area.
The left panel renders a six-stage SVG timeline with month markers, a vertical spine, dashed elbow connectors, arrow markers, and gold-bordered stage boxes.
The right panel contains the title sequence, two lead statements, an Entry Markets section, and three stat groups for Segment One, Segment Two, and Segment Three.
Styling relies heavily on CSS custom properties from /surface/styles/vars.css plus local variables such as --zone.
Blueprint grid accents appear in the upper left and upper right using masked repeating gradients.
A footer grounding band is first defined as a taller labeled band, then overridden by a v2.1 correction into a thin quiet foot that hides its children.
The circular V mark is positioned near the lower right and lifted by the v2.1 correction.
standouts: Split composition pairing a full-height timeline zone with a text-and-stat right panel.
Inline SVG handles the timeline geometry directly inside the document.
Late CSS overrides document a variant correction without removing the earlier footer-band implementation.
conventions: Single-file HTML document with embedded CSS and inline SVG.
Uses design-token CSS variables for spacing, type, color, and surfaces.
Keeps the main title left-aligned as noted by an inline comment.
Uses semantic class names for visual regions and repeated stat blocks rather than componentized markup.
Breaks the initial footer-band styling through later !important overrides in the same style block.
concerns: The fixed 1600px by 900px viewport is not responsive within this file.
The gband and mark rules are redefined later with !important, leaving superseded styling in place.
The footer band still contains hidden markup and copy after the v2.1 correction hides all children.
Inline SVG dimensions and coordinates are hard-coded, making content changes manual.
External font and stylesheet dependencies are required for the intended rendering.
notes: A slide-style split-zone rollout layout with a staged month timeline on the left and market-entry statistics on the right.
questions: Should the hidden footer-band label remain in the DOM after the v2.1 correction?
Are the stage labels placeholders or intended final copy?
Should the fixed 1600x900 canvas be preserved for presentation output only?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated title and comments describe a split zone, cascade timeline, and stat groups; the file implements those elements directly.
intention_signals: It seems intended to present a strategic rollout as a phased timeline tied to quantified entry-market segments, with a warm planning-board aesthetic.
novelty: The file combines a full-bleed warm timeline zone, masked blueprint corner grids, dashed SVG elbow routing, and a corrected thin grounding band in one static slide composition.
contribution: This file contributes a split presentation pattern that keeps a left-side SVG cascade timeline, right-side narrative and grouped metrics, blueprint-corner texture, token-based warm color treatment, and a subdued footer ground line for a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS presentation view for Var 12 showing a two-column strategic rollout slide with a six-stage SVG month timeline and grouped entry-market statistics.
intention_for_embedding: Created to express a phased ecosystem rollout over 24 months using a split-zone visual structure with timeline sequencing and market metric groups.
